name: 昆明
description: Kunming, the capital of Yunnan province, is known as the 'Spring City' for its pleasant climate year-round. Located at an elevation of 1,890 meters, it serves as the political, economic, and cultural center of Yunnan. The city is a gateway to stunning natural landscapes including the UNESCO World Heritage Site of Stone Forest, and is home to 26 ethnic minorities preserving rich traditions.
highlights: Dianchi Lake - Largest lake in Yunnan
Golden Horse and Phoenix Gate - Iconic archway

climate.tips: Kunming is called 'Spring City' with mild weather year-round. Summer is rainy season (June-August) but not hot. Winter is dry and sunny. Bring layers year-round as temperatures vary daily. UV is strong due to high altitude.

attractions.name: 石林
attractions.description: UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring thousands of limestone pillars formed over 270 million years, creating a dramatic forest-like landscape of stone formations.
attractions.address: Stone Forest Scenic Area, Shilin County, Yunnan
attractions.tips: Arrive early to avoid tour groups. Wear comfortable walking shoes. The area is large - rent an electric car or hire a local guide. Combine with nearby Jiuxiang Scenic Area.

attractions.name: 滇池
attractions.description: The largest lake in Yunnan and sixth largest freshwater lake in China, offering scenic views of the city skyline and Kunming's 'West Lake' experience.
attractions.tips: Best visited at sunrise or sunset for dramatic photos. Haigeng Park has beautiful gardens. The lake is polluted - don't swim or drink. Winter brings migratory birds.

attractions.name: 云南民族村
attractions.description: A theme park showcasing the customs, architecture, and traditions of Yunnan's 26 ethnic minorities in their authentic village settings.
attractions.tips: Arrive when gates open for best experience. Daily performance schedule available at entrance. Allow 3+ hours minimum. Combine with nearby Western Hills.

attractions.name: 翠湖
attractions.description: An urban oasis in central Kunming featuring a scenic lake, lotus ponds, and historic pavilions, popular with locals for morning tai chi and散步.
attractions.tips: Best in early morning for tai chi and traditional music. Summer has beautiful lotus flowers. Surrounding streets have good coffee shops. Connected to Yunnan University area.

attractions.name: 金马碧鸡坊
attractions.description: The iconic symbol of Kunming featuring two ornate archways built in 1381 during the Ming Dynasty, representing the legendary golden horse and phoenix.
attractions.tips: Most impressive at night when illuminated. Located near downtown shopping areas. Good starting point for exploring Kunming's old streets. Watch for traffic when taking photos.

attractions.name: 昆明世界园艺博览园
attractions.description: Built for the 1999 World Horticultural Exposition, featuring beautiful gardens from around China and world, plus permanent international pavilions.
attractions.tips: Spring and autumn best for flower viewing. Large area - allow half day. Combine with nearby Golden Temple. Great for photography.

attractions.name: 黑龙潭
attractions.description: A historic Taoist temple complex with ancient plum trees, lotus ponds, and the famous 'Three Immortals' cypress trees over 500 years old.
attractions.tips: Best visited during plum blossom season (January-February). Beautiful lotus in summer. Less crowded than major attractions. Famous for plum flower painting.

attractions.name: 大观公园
attractions.description: Famous for the 'First Scenery of Yunnan' ink painting displayed in the historic Daguan Lou pavilion, plus lakeside gardens and the 1800m long corridor.
attractions.tips: Visit the pavilion for the famous 'First Scenery of Yunnan' painting (replica). Best in spring for flower shows. Good for sunset photos over Dianchi Lake.

attractions.name: 金殿
attractions.description: Yunnan's largest Taoist temple built in 1602, featuring China's largest pure gold-covered copper structure and ancient bronze statues.
attractions.tips: Built entirely of brass and gold - the original 1602 structure. Take cable car up for views. Combine with Black Dragon Pool nearby. Spring plum blossoms are beautiful.

attractions.name: 西山龙门
attractions.description: A scenic mountain area overlooking Dianchi Lake with hiking trails, Buddhist temples, and the famous Dragon Gate carved into cliffside.
attractions.tips: Take bus 51 or taxi from downtown. Combine with nearby Yunnan Ethnic Village. Wear hiking shoes for forest trails. Allow full morning or afternoon.

attractions.name: 昆明老街
attractions.description: Historic streets preserving traditional Yunnan architecture with local shops, traditional medicine stores, and authentic local snacks.
attractions.tips: Great for buying Yunnan specialties. Sample local snacks like crossing-bridge rice noodles. Evening has street food vendors. bargaining expected.

attractions.name: 云南师范大学
attractions.description: Beautiful campus with historic buildings from the '国立西南联大' era when Peking, Tsinghua and Nankai universities relocated during WWII.
attractions.tips: Beautiful in spring for cherry blossoms. The wartime history is fascinating. Near Cuihu Park - combine visits. Respect students during class hours.

attractions.name: 双塔景区
attractions.description: Two ancient Buddhist pagodas dating to the Tang Dynasty (9th century), standing 43 meters tall as the oldest surviving architecture in Kunming.
attractions.tips: Less crowded than other attractions. Climb to top for panoramic views. Evening lighting makes for dramatic photos. Buddhist festivals bring special events.

attractions.name: 圆通寺
attractions.description: The largest Buddhist temple in Kunming and one of the oldest in Yunnan, featuring a unique architectural design with a circular hall in the center.
attractions.tips: Active Buddhist temple - dress modestly. Popular with locals on Buddhist holidays. Good location near Cuihu area. Try vegetarian food nearby.

attractions.name: 锦庭花海
attractions.description: A vast flower farm with seasonal blooms including lavender, sunflowers, and rapeseed flowers, popular for photography and rural scenery.
attractions.tips: Best seasons: spring and autumn. Wear a hat and sunscreen. Combine with farm-stay experiences. Great for Instagram-style photos.

attractions.name: 美联生态园
attractions.description: An ecological garden showcasing Yunnan tropical plants and flowers in climate-controlled greenhouses, with ethnic performances.
attractions.tips: Good for rainy days. The tropical greenhouse is impressive. Check show schedule for timing. Near Golden Temple area.

attractions.name: 通海秀山
attractions.description: A scenic area combining Buddhist and Taoist temples on a forested mountain with views of Tonghai Lake and ancient cultural relics.
attractions.tips: Combine with visit to Tonghai county. The temples have beautiful carvings. Hiking takes 2-3 hours. Bring water and snacks.

attractions.name: 石林古镇
attractions.description: An ancient Yi ethnic minority town near Stone Forest, preserving traditional architecture, local markets, and ethnic customs.
attractions.tips: Visit before or after Stone Forest. The local Yi market is colorful. Buy ethnic handicrafts here rather than at Stone Forest. Sample Yi cuisine.

attractions.name: 九乡景区
attractions.description: An underground cave system featuring stalactites, underground rivers, and dramatic karst formations dubbed 'The Underwater World of Yunnan'.
attractions.tips: Combine with Stone Forest in same day. Boat ride through cave is unique. Wear non-slip shoes for wet cave floors. Temperature inside is cool.

attractions.name: 龙门飞甲瀑
attractions.description: A scenic waterfall area with hiking trails, natural swimming holes, and beautiful canyon scenery in the eastern suburbs of Kunming.
attractions.tips: Summer best for swimming. Bring swimwear and towel. Hiking shoes recommended. Arrive early on weekends to find parking.

attractions.name: 玉华寺
attractions.description: Historic Buddhist nunnery in western Kunming with beautiful gardens, ancient pagodas, and peaceful atmosphere away from tourist crowds.
attractions.tips: Less visited than other temples. Beautiful in spring for plum blossoms. Combine with nearby Daguan Park area.

attractions.name: 石林彝族服饰街
attractions.description: Colorful market street in Shilin selling authentic Yi ethnic costumes, silver jewelry, and traditional handicrafts.
attractions.tips: Best after visiting Stone Forest. Compare prices before buying. The silver items are authentic. Good for souvenirs.

attractions.name: 滇池长廊
attractions.description: Scenic lakefront promenade with gardens, exercise areas, and views of Dianchi Lake and Western Hills.
attractions.tips: Best at sunrise for photos. Popular with locals doing exercise. Rent a bike for exploring. Bring sunscreen.

attractions.name: 昆明动物博物馆
attractions.description: Excellent natural history museum showcasing Yunnan's biodiversity including butterflies, birds, and mammals from the region.
attractions.tips: Good for rainy days. The butterfly collection is outstanding. Allow 2-3 hours. Combine with nearby Green Lake area.

attractions.name: 同仁街
attractions.description: Historic commercial street from the 1920s-30s with colonial-style buildings, now featuring cafes, galleries, and boutique shops.
attractions.tips: Great for afternoon coffee. The architecture is unique in Kunming. Good for photos. Evening lighting is romantic.

attractions.name: 赢财寺
attractions.description: Ancient Buddhist temple on a hillside with thousand-year-old trees, stone carvings, and panoramic views of Kunming.
attractions.tips: Off the beaten path. Hiking path to temple is pleasant. The old banyan trees are impressive. Best visited in morning.

attractions.name: 华华路夜市
attractions.description: Popular night market with local street food, souvenirs, and shopping in a vibrant atmosphere near Cuihu Park.
attractions.tips: Best after 7pm. Great for trying Yunnan street food. Bargaining expected. Try the grilled cheese and local beer.

attractions.name: 双桥生态园
attractions.description: Urban ecological park with wetlands, bird watching areas, and walking trails along the Panlong River.
attractions.tips: Best for nature lovers. Bring binoculars for bird watching. Morning is best for birds. Good for escaping city crowds.

attractions.name: 云南陆军讲武堂
attractions.description: Historic military academy where Zhu De,叶剑英and other Chinese leaders studied, now a museum with excellent exhibits.
attractions.tips: Free admission. Allow 1-2 hours. The architecture is beautiful. Good introduction to Chinese revolutionary history.

attractions.name: 安宁温泉
attractions.description: Famous hot spring resort area 40km from Kunming, known for its mineral-rich waters and historic spa culture.
attractions.tips: Best for relaxation. Several resorts to choose from. Bring swimwear and towel. Combine with day trip. Weekday visits are quieter.

restaurants.name: 过桥园（光华街店）
restaurants.description: Famous for authentic Crossing Bridge Rice Noodles (过桥米线) with rich broth and fresh ingredients served in traditional style.
restaurants.tips: Try the 'grandmother's recipe' set. Lunch is crowded - arrive before noon. Ask for less spicy if sensitive.

restaurants.name: 川菜佬
restaurants.description: Popular Sichuan restaurant serving authentic spicy dishes including dan dan noodles, mapo tofu, and spicy crayfish.
restaurants.tips: Very spicy - specify spice level when ordering. Dinner reservation recommended. Try the cold dishes as appetizers.

restaurants.name: 大理人家（昆明店）
restaurants.description: Bai ethnic cuisine restaurant recreating traditional Dali dishes with local Yunnan ingredients and Bai cooking methods.
restaurants.tips: Good introduction to Yunnan ethnic cuisine. The raw meat salad is a specialty. Ask about the 'three-course tea' ceremony.

restaurants.name: 昆明老街夜市
restaurants.description: Bustling night market on historic Kunming Old Street featuring local snacks, grilled meats, and regional specialties.
restaurants.tips: Best after 7pm. Try a bit of everything - small portions. Bring cash. Watch for food hygiene. Great for atmosphere.

restaurants.name: 小云南
restaurants.description: Upscale Yunnan cuisine restaurant featuring wild mushroom dishes, minority specialties, and Yunnan herbs in elegant setting.
restaurants.tips: Best in rainy season for fresh mushrooms. Reservation required. The steam pot chicken is legendary. Expensive but excellent quality.

restaurants.name: 老昆明酒楼
restaurants.description: Classic Kunming restaurant serving traditional local dishes with recipes dating back 50+ years in nostalgic setting.
restaurants.tips: Local institution. The fried rice is famous citywide. Good for first-time Yunnan food explorers. Lunch sets are good value.

restaurants.name: 青岩民族菜
restaurants.description: Restaurant specializing in dishes from various Yunnan ethnic minorities including Yi, Miao, Zhuang, and Bai.
restaurants.tips: Good variety for trying multiple ethnic cuisines. Groups should order the 'ethnic sampler'. Very flavorful and spicy.

restaurants.name: 花菇娘野生菌
restaurants.description: Premium wild mushroom hotpot restaurant with safety-certified mushrooms, serving seasonal varieties from Yunnan forests.
restaurants.tips: Only open during mushroom season (June-November). Price varies by mushroom type. Always confirm mushroom safety. Book in advance.

restaurants.name: 川江号子火锅
restaurants.description: Popular chain hotpot restaurant with authentic Sichuan mala (numbing-spicy) broth and quality meat slices.
restaurants.tips: Very popular - expect waits on weekends. All-you-can-eat options available. The broth is very spicy. Bring change of clothes.

restaurants.name: 云南汽锅鸡
restaurants.description: Traditional restaurant specializing in steam pot chicken (汽锅鸡) cooked using traditional clay pots and Yunnan herbs.
restaurants.tips: The broth is the highlight - drink it like soup. Allow 30 minutes cooking time. Multiple locations throughout city.

restaurants.name: 蜀香民族菜
restaurants.description: Authentic Yi ethnic cuisine with famous spicy dishes and traditional cooking methods from the Yi people.
restaurants.tips: Very local - English menu unlikely. Point and choose from kitchen. The spicy chicken is addictive. Good for adventurous eaters.

restaurants.name: 春城味道
restaurants.description: Modern Yunnan fusion restaurant combining traditional recipes with contemporary presentation in elegant setting.
restaurants.tips: Best for visitors wanting refined Yunnan cuisine. Instagram-worthy presentation. Good for special occasions. Reservation recommended.

restaurants.name: 德居火锅
restaurants.description: Authentic Chongqing-style hotpot with thick mala broth, ox stomach, and various organ meats popular with locals.
restaurants.tips: Not for the faint-hearted - very authentic offal-heavy menu. Specify spice level. Local favorite - expect crowds.

restaurants.name: 彼得厨房
restaurants.description: Western Yunnan cuisine specialist focusing on dishes from Dali, Lijiang, and Shangri-La regions with authentic recipes.
restaurants.tips: Good alternative if tired of generic Yunnan food. The yak meat is lean and healthy. Portion sizes are generous. English menu available.

restaurants.name: 林花谷私房菜
restaurants.description: Exclusive private kitchen experience with chef's tasting menu featuring seasonal Yunnan ingredients and innovative dishes.
restaurants.tips: Must book 1 day ahead. Only 8 tables available. Perfect for special occasions. Dietary restrictions can be accommodated.

restaurants.name: 川香豆花庄
restaurants.description: Casual restaurant famous for tofu pudding (豆花) dishes both savory and sweet, plus Sichuan-Yunnan noodle dishes.
restaurants.tips: Great breakfast spot. The tofu pudding desserts are unique. Open early - good before sightseeing. Very cheap.

restaurants.name: 白亚烤肉
restaurants.description: Popular Korean BBQ restaurant with quality beef and pork, great banchan (side dishes), and all-you-can-eat options.
restaurants.tips: Good air conditioning - escape summer heat. All-you-can-eat is good value. Dinner reservation needed on weekends.

restaurants.name: 新营家乡菜
restaurants.description: Home-style Yunnan cooking with emphasis on fresh seasonal ingredients and traditional recipes from Kunming area.
restaurants.tips: No-frills local restaurant. Point at ingredients in kitchen. Rice is unlimited. Very popular with office workers at lunch.

restaurants.name: 云南花意式餐厅
restaurants.description: Italian restaurant in Kunming using Yunnan local ingredients like wild mushrooms and herbs in traditional Italian recipes.
restaurants.tips: Great for Western food cravings. Fusion menu is interesting. Romantic atmosphere. Pricey by Yunnan standards.

restaurants.name: 民升美食广场
restaurants.description: Multi-vendor food court in department store with various Yunnan and Chinese fast food options at reasonable prices.
restaurants.tips: Good for quick, cheap meals. Each vendor has own opening hours. AC available. Easy to find in city center.

restaurants.name: 云南山厨房
restaurants.description: Restaurant specializing in mountain cuisine from Yunnan's highlands including wild vegetables, river fish, and highland herbs.
restaurants.tips: Great for trying mountain cuisine. The river fish is excellent. Ask for chef's recommendations. Good for groups.

restaurants.name: 傣味轩
restaurants.description: Restaurant serving Dai ethnic cuisine from Xishuangbanna with sour and spicy flavors, grilled meats, and tropical ingredients.
restaurants.tips: The sour soup is excellent. Bring friends to try variety of dishes. Very flavorful. Good for spicy food lovers.

restaurants.name: 南屏街小吃
restaurants.description: Historic commercial street with local snack vendors, Yunnan specialties, and bustling night market atmosphere.
restaurants.tips: Best in evening. Try small portions from multiple vendors. Very local atmosphere. Great for casual eating.

restaurants.name: 春山美餐厅
restaurants.description: Modern Yunnan fusion restaurant with creative dishes using local ingredients, elegant presentation, and contemporary setting.
restaurants.tips: Best for special occasions. The dishes are Instagram-worthy. Reservation required for dinner. Pricey but worth it.

restaurants.name: 广通传统菜馆
restaurants.description: Local favorite serving traditional Yunnan dishes with recipes passed down for generations, known for honest portions.
restaurants.tips: Very popular with locals. Point at kitchen display for ordering. The portions are large. Good for authentic food.

hotels.name: 云举大酒店
hotels.description: 5-star luxury hotel near Cuihu Park with panoramic city views, spa, multiple restaurants, and premium service.
hotels.tips: Best views from executive floors. Book direct for better rates. Breakfast buffet is excellent.

hotels.name: 昆明饭店
hotels.description: Historic 5-star hotel in central Kunming, known for excellent service and convenient location near shopping and attractions.
hotels.tips: Long history - many Chinese leaders stayed here. Excellent location for first-time visitors. Book well in advance.

hotels.name: 金格大酒店
hotels.description: 4-star hotel near Golden Horse and Phoenix Gate with comfortable rooms, good service, and proximity to major attractions.
hotels.tips: Best value in this category. Check for weekend discounts. Near metro station. Staff speak some English.

hotels.name: 翠湖宾馆
hotels.description: Boutique hotel overlooking Cuihu Park with artistic decor, excellent restaurant, and personalized service.
hotels.tips: Best for romantic getaways. The brunch is famous citywide. Very quiet despite central location. Book park-view rooms.

hotels.name: 凯拉斯酒店
hotels.description: Modern mid-range hotel near shopping district with comfortable rooms, reliable service, and good breakfast.
hotels.tips: Good balance of price and quality. Metro station 5 min walk. Breakfast included in most rates.

hotels.name: 云南商务酒店
hotels.description: Reliable business hotel near Kunming's business district with comfortable rooms and efficient service.
hotels.tips: Popular with business travelers. Get floor higher up for views. Weekend rates often discounted.

hotels.name: 春城客栈
hotels.description: Charming budget hotel near Cuihu Park with Yunnan ethnic decor, clean rooms, and local character.
hotels.tips: Best budget option near Cuihu. Book early as small hotel. English-speaking staff limited.

hotels.name: 昆明青年旅舍
hotels.description: Popular backpacker hostel near Kunming Railway Station with dorms, private rooms, and social atmosphere.
hotels.tips: Best for solo travelers. Evening social events. Very close to railway station. Noisy at times.

hotels.name: 东华大酒店
hotels.description: Modern 4-star hotel near East Kunming with comfortable rooms, good restaurants, and comprehensive facilities.
hotels.tips: Good for those with cars. Far from center but good metro connection. Newer property - fresh decor.

hotels.name: 西山度假酒店
hotels.description: Resort hotel on the Western Hills with scenic views of Dianchi Lake, forest setting, and fresh air.
hotels.tips: Best for nature lovers. Rent a car recommended. Quiet and peaceful. Good for weekend getaways.

hotels.name: 丽江饭店
hotels.description: Nostalgic hotel with Naxi-style architecture and decor, offering unique character near city center.
hotels.tips: Unique architectural style. Good for experiencing Yunnan culture. Clean but older facilities.

hotels.name: 公园1903精品酒店
hotels.description: French-inspired boutique hotel in the Park 1903 development with elegant rooms and upscale amenities.
hotels.tips: Best for romantic stays. The surrounding Park 1903 area is nice for strolling. Dinner reservation needed for restaurant.

hotels.name: 昆明机场酒店
hotels.description: Convenient hotel directly connected to Kunming Changshui Airport terminal, ideal for transit passengers.
hotels.tips: Best for early morning flights or late arrivals. Book directly with hotel for better rate. Shower facilities available.

hotels.name: 盘龙酒店
hotels.description: Comfortable mid-range hotel near Panlong River with modern rooms, good breakfast, and practical amenities.
hotels.tips: Good value for money. Metro station 10 min walk. Rooms on higher floors have better views.

hotels.name: 金都酒店
hotels.description: Clean and affordable budget hotel near Kunming Railway Station with basic but comfortable rooms.
hotels.tips: Best budget option near station. Many long-distance bus connections nearby. Room quality varies - request newer floor.

hotels.name: 红塔公园酒店
hotels.description: Upscale hotel near Hongta Park with modern facilities, multiple dining options, and professional service.
hotels.tips: Good for both business and leisure. Nearby park is nice for morning walks. Weekend discounts available.

hotels.name: 楚雄酒店
hotels.description: Comfortable mid-range hotel near Kunming's business district with clean rooms and practical amenities.
hotels.tips: Good value for money. Near highway entrance. Weekend discounts available. Rooms are well-maintained.

hotels.name: 小云南精品客栈
hotels.description: Ethnic-themed boutique hotel with Yunnan's minority culture decoration, offering unique local experience.
hotels.tips: Best for cultural experience. The decoration is impressive. Near Cuihu Park. Book early for best rooms.

hotels.name: 天城酒店
hotels.description: Modern business hotel near Kunming's financial district with comfortable rooms and good meeting facilities.
hotels.tips: Good for business travelers. The rooms are modern and clean. Weekend discounts available. Near metro station.

hotels.name: 石林彝族客栈
hotels.description: Ethnic Yi-style hotel in Shilin County near Stone Forest, featuring traditional architecture and local character.
hotels.tips: Best for visiting Stone Forest area. The ethnic decoration is authentic. Book early during peak season. Combine with ancient town visit.

emergencyContacts.name: 昆明市公安局出入境管理处
昆明市气象局
emergencyContacts.description: Weather forecasts and severe weather warnings

emergencyContacts.name: 昆明市森林防火指挥部
昆明市燃气公司
emergencyContacts.description: Gas leak and gas emergency reporting

culturalTips.title: 云南民族多样性
culturalTips.content: 昆明是云南26个少数民族的汇聚地，汉族占多数外，还有彝族、回族、白族、苗族、傣族等。尊重各民族的习俗和禁忌。

culturalTips.title: 寺庙礼仪
culturalTips.content: 参观佛教或道教寺庙时，穿着要得体（遮盖肩膀和膝盖）。不要用手指佛像，保持安静。点燃香火后要用手扇灭而不是吹灭。

culturalTips.title: 昆明集市还价
culturalTips.content: 在昆明老街或民族村购买手工艺品时，还价是正常的文化习惯。通常可以还到标价的30-50%。保持友好态度，以笑容开始。

culturalTips.title: 春城天气特点
culturalTips.content: 昆明四季如春，但紫外线强（海拔1890米），即使阴天也要涂防晒霜。早晚温差大，夏季也要带薄外套。

culturalTips.title: 过桥米线文化
culturalTips.content: 过桥米线是云南著名美食，据说源于昆明。吃法讲究：先放生肉，再放蔬菜，最后放米线。汤很烫，不要急于喝。

culturalTips.title: 昆明方言
culturalTips.content: 昆明话属于云南方言，语调与普通话差异较大。当地人很热情，即使你用普通话交流也不会有问题。

culturalTips.title: 高原注意事项
culturalTips.content: 昆明海拔约1890米，一般不会有高原反应。但如果前往石林或西山徒步，建议多喝水，避免剧烈运动。

culturalTips.title: 云南普洱茶文化
culturalTips.content: 云南是普洱茶产地，昆明有很多茶馆。购买茶叶时可以先品茶，询问茶叶产地和年份。好的普洱越陈越香。

culturalTips.title: 花城昆明
culturalTips.content: 昆明被称为'花城'，全年有鲜花。斗南花卉市场是亚洲最大的花卉交易市场，可以买便宜鲜花带回家。

culturalTips.title: 昆明夜市购物
culturalTips.content: 昆明老街夜市的商品质量参差不齐。购买民族服饰和手工艺品时，记得货比三家并适当还价。

culturalTips.title: 云南辣味
culturalTips.content: 云南菜系偏辣，但不是四川那种麻辣。如果不吃辣，点菜时明确说'不要辣'。昆明餐厅一般都能调整辣度。

culturalTips.title: 昆明公交文化
culturalTips.content: 昆明公交系统发达，但高峰期很拥挤。让座给老人和孕妇是基本礼仪。上车要排队，不要拥挤。

culturalTips.title: 民族节日
culturalTips.content: 昆明及其周边有多个少数民族节日，如火把节（彝族）、泼水节（傣族）等。参加时请尊重当地习俗和宗教信仰。

culturalTips.title: 野生菌食用安全
culturalTips.content: 云南野生菌种类繁多，6-11月是旺季。只在正规餐厅食用，不要自行采摘。野生菌中毒很危险，绝不要吃不认识的菌类。

culturalTips.title: 昆明商务礼仪
culturalTips.content: 昆明商业文化较为务实。见面时握手是常见礼仪，交换名片用双手。商务宴请通常会持续较长时间。

culturalTips.title: 摄影建议
culturalTips.content: 拍摄当地人或民族服饰前，建议先征得同意。寺庙内拍照前先看指示牌，有些区域禁止拍照。

culturalTips.title: 云南本地酒文化
culturalTips.content: 云南本地有包谷酒、梅子酒等。昆明人待客热情，敬酒是常见礼仪。酒量因人而异，不要勉强自己。

culturalTips.title: 过桥米线的传说
culturalTips.content: 传说过桥米线源于一位书生妻子送饭过桥的故事，汤要用滚油保温以保持温度。食用时先放生肉再放蔬菜最后放米线。

culturalTips.title: 云南鲜花入菜
culturalTips.content: 云南有食用鲜花的传统，玫瑰、桂花、茉莉等都可入菜。昆明菜市场有各种食用花卉出售，是独特的美食体验。

culturalTips.title: 汽锅鸡的烹饪传统
culturalTips.content: 汽锅鸡用特制土陶汽锅蒸制，利用蒸汽循环保持鸡肉原汁原味。加入三七、天麻等药材可增强滋补功效。

culturalTips.title: 石林撒尼文化
culturalTips.content: 石林是彝族撒尼人聚居地，他们有独特的跳月舞和阿诗玛传说。观看撒尼人表演时，请尊重他们的文化习俗。

culturalTips.title: 滇池生态保护
culturalTips.content: 滇池是昆明的母亲湖，但曾经污染严重，现在正在恢复中。请不要在湖中游泳或乱扔垃圾，保护湖泊生态。

culturalTips.title: 云南小粒咖啡
culturalTips.content: 云南是中国咖啡主产地，昆明有很多特色咖啡馆。普洱和德宏产的小粒咖啡品质优良，是伴手礼的好选择。

culturalTips.title: 翠湖晨练文化
culturalTips.content: 翠湖是昆明人最爱的晨练场所，每天早晨都有市民在湖边打太极、跳广场舞、唱歌。游客可以观摩但请保持安静。

culturalTips.title: 云南温泉文化
culturalTips.content: 云南地热资源丰富，温泉是当地重要的休闲方式。安宁温泉是著名温泉胜地，水质优良，适合休闲度假。

payment.name: 支付宝
payment.description: Most widely accepted mobile payment in Kunming
tips: Download app and link bank card before arrival. Works at most stores, restaurants, and hotels.
howToUse: Download app and link bank card before arrival. Works at most stores, restaurants, and hotels.

payment.name: 微信支付
payment.description: Second most popular mobile payment, essential for WeChat communication
tips: WeChat is used for everything in China. WeChat Pay is integrated. Both Alipay and WeChat Pay are accepted almost everywhere.
howToUse: WeChat is used for everything in China. WeChat Pay is integrated. Both Alipay and WeChat Pay are accepted almost everywhere.

payment.name: 现金
payment.description: Cash still accepted at markets, small shops, and rural areas
tips: Carry 200-500¥ cash for markets and small vendors. ATMs widely available. Rural areas may not accept cards.
howToUse: Carry 200-500¥ cash for markets and small vendors. ATMs widely available. Rural areas may not accept cards.

payment.name: 银联卡
payment.description: China's dominant card network, accepted at most merchants
tips: Check your bank allows international transactions. Inform bank of travel plans before departure. Most ATMs accept foreign cards.
howToUse: Check your bank allows international transactions. Inform bank of travel plans before departure. Most ATMs accept foreign cards.

payment.name: Visa/Mastercard
payment.description: International credit cards accepted at large hotels and upscale restaurants only
tips: Don't rely on international cards in Kunming. Most small merchants don't accept them. China Merchants Bank and HSBC ATMs sometimes work.
howToUse: Don't rely on international cards in Kunming. Most small merchants don't accept them. China Merchants Bank and HSBC ATMs sometimes work.

payment.name: 旅行支票
tips: Traveler's cheques are not recommended. Exchange them at banks if you have them. Cash and mobile payment are much more practical.
howToUse: Traveler's cheques are not recommended. Exchange them at banks if you have them. Cash and mobile payment are much more practical.

arrival.name: 昆明长水国际机场
arrival.description: Main international airport serving Kunming, located 25km from city center
arrival.toCityCenter: Airport metro line 6 (40 min, 5¥) or taxi (60-80¥, 40-60 min depending on traffic)

arrival.name: 昆明站
arrival.description: Main railway station in city center with connections to most major Chinese cities
arrival.toCityCenter: Walking distance to city center (15 min) or metro lines 1/2 (5 min)

arrival.name: 昆明南站
arrival.description: High-speed rail hub in呈贡新城 area for G/D trains
arrival.toCityCenter: Metro line 1 (45 min to city center, 4¥) or taxi (80-100¥, 50-70 min)

arrival.name: 昆明东部客运站
arrival.description: Bus station for routes to eastern Yunnan including Stone Forest
arrival.toCityCenter: Metro line 3 (25 min) or taxi (30-50¥, 30-45 min)

arrival.name: 昆明西北部客运站
arrival.description: Bus station for routes to western Yunnan including Dali and Lijiang

bus: Extensive bus network covering all city areas
taxi: Didi app works well for English users with translation
Insist on using meter in regular taxis
Airport/hotel taxis may charge fixed rates
rental: Scan QR code with WeChat or Alipay to unlock
Best for flat areas near Cuihu
International license may not be accepted - check requirements
Traffic can be congested in city center
Best for day trips to Stone Forest or Western Hills

quickFacts.water: Tap water is boiled before drinking; bottled water widely available
